title: Glycine multijuga
taxonomy: Glycine multijuga is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This classification places it in the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Galegeae. Its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants within the same family.

intro: Glycine multijuga is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, commonly known as the "Chinese kidney bean." Traditionally, Glycine multijuga has been used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Glycine multijuga include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Glycine multijuga is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make tonics and other herbal preparations.
